name: Final Authority in AI Governance: Frontier-Provider Sovereignty and Action-Centered Deployer Governance
summary: arXiv:2607.13040v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: This paper examines where final authority should sit once capable AI systems are embedded in organizational workflows. It compares two governance m...
description: arXiv:2607.13040v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: This paper examines where final authority should sit once capable AI systems are embedded in organizational workflows. It compares two governance models. The first, frontier-provider sovereignty, assigns privileged authority to the provider of the most capable models and is reflected in contemporary arguments for frontier-model testing, release...
author: Zexun Wang
